mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45% over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in residential and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns during the specified regime are negative or statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.5), or the signal reverses direction in subsequent recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates a negative real yield environment that triggers equity risk premium repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined condition yields positive 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.0 regardless of the funds rate level.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 36-month windows, or payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth alongside declining housing starts during a credit-constrained expansion signals balance sheet deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth falls below 1.0%, or the hypothesis is rejected if housing starts remain stable while GDP stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 100.0 index baseline confirms sustained manufacturing strength that supports near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 100.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.
